• Individuals passionate about building products with real-world impact.
• Applicants willing to learn rapidly and take ownership.
Selection Criteria
We seek highly talented and bright individuals with exceptional potential.
Selection is based on:
• Demonstrated capability
• Portfolio quality
• Problem-solving ability
• Creativity and innovation
• Execution excellence
• Learning agility
• Character and professionalism
Institution, degree, titles, and prior affiliations are secondary to demonstrated merit and capability.

• Opportunity for fast-track leadership and extended engagement opportunities.

title: Chief of Staff — Founder's Office
function: Founder's Office Strategy, Execution & Cross-functional Leverage
location: Remote / Hybrid (India preferred) — willingness to travel for strategic meetings
about:
EduRankAI is a frontier AI and educational intelligence organization working at the intersection of artificial intelligence, research, talent development, and institutional transformation. The Chief of Staff sits inside the Founder's Office and is the highest-leverage non-functional role in the company.

This is not an administrative role. It is the seat where strategy meets execution, where ambiguous problems get resolved into shippable plans, and where the founder's bandwidth is multiplied by someone who can be trusted with anything from a board memo to a hiring loop to a partner negotiation. The Chief of Staff has no formal team and unlimited scope.

The right person for this role will look at the company in three months and see their fingerprint on at least six different functions — and at six months will have made themselves indispensable in ways that did not exist in the original job description.

You'll thrive here if: you treat ambiguity as a resource, not a problem. You read drafts of other people's writing for sport. You can run a hiring loop in the morning, a partner call at lunch, and a market-sizing analysis in the afternoon — and you would rather do that than specialize. You understand that the highest-leverage role in a frontier company is the one nobody wrote a job spec for, and you can write that spec for yourself.

You'll struggle here if: you measure your impact by org-chart depth. You need a clear remit handed to you. You think founders should outsource judgment. You write memos that nobody opens.
responsibilities: Work directly with the Founder on strategic and organizational priorities — daily.
Drive execution of high-impact initiatives across multiple functions (research, AI platform, product, partnerships, hiring).
Conduct first-principles research and analysis on markets, technology, education, policy, partnerships, and organizational development.
Coordinate cross-functional projects and own delivery against deadlines that the rest of the company is watching.
Develop executive briefings, board memos, public communications, and decision-support materials — at a quality the Founder would publish under their own name.
Support institutional partnerships, stakeholder engagement, and strategic collaborations (governments, universities, donor agencies, research labs).
Lead leadership hiring loops alongside the People function — sourcing, calibration, panel design, closing.
Identify operational bottlenecks across the company and design scalable solutions you then drive to implementation.
Monitor progress against organizational goals and KPIs; surface drift early.
Hold confidential and mission-critical projects requiring discretion and sound judgment.
skills: Strong written + verbal communication — writes well enough that the Founder would publish your drafts under their name
Sound judgment and professional maturity under pressure
Comfort operating in ambiguous and rapidly changing environments
Strong stakeholder management — internal leadership AND external partners (government, university, donor, board)
Intellectual curiosity that survives professional context
Polyglot fluency across at least three of: strategy, ops, finance, product, research, partnerships, hiring
eligibility: You have founded a company, run a strategy or operations function at a serious startup, or held a Chief of Staff role at a frontier-AI or frontier-tech organization. Equivalent paths considered.
You can describe in writing a $50k+ decision you made (or strongly influenced) and what the second-order consequence was.
You write memos that other people open and forward. You can point at one.
You have run a hiring loop end-to-end (sourcing, calibration, panel, close) and you have hired at least two Senior or Staff-tier people who stayed past 18 months.
You can read a research paper, identify the load-bearing claim in 30 minutes, and brief a non-technical reader on it in 200 words.
You hold contradictions productively — you can argue for two opposing positions in writing and articulate where each is right.
You are comfortable being the most junior person in the room AND the most senior person on the call, often in the same hour.

title: Campus Ambassador (Student Programme)
about:
EduRankAI is hiring Campus Ambassadors at universities and colleges across India and beyond. The role is the bridge between your campus and the company. You will run on-ground awareness for EduRankAI's open roles, AquinTutor learning programmes, internships, hackathons, and research opportunities — and you will be the first person at your campus that students hear about us from.

This is a paid, structured, semester-long programme with a clear ladder. The strongest ambassadors are invited to internships, employment, and research opportunities at the end of their tenure — and many of our Ambassadors graduate directly into Junior or Mid roles at EduRankAI.

What you get (full facility list):
• Monthly stipend, ₹5,000–₹15,000 / month, tiered by campus + tenure, paid into your bank account.
• Performance bonuses for events, content shipped, and qualified candidate referrals.
• Official Campus Ambassador certificate at semester end, signed by the Founder.
• EduRankAI swag pack (t-shirt + notebook + stickers) shipped to you.
• Letter of Recommendation from your manager on successful completion.
• Priority track for EduRankAI internships and full-time roles at the end of tenure.
• Free lifetime access to AquinTutor Premium for personal learning.
• Early access to EduRankAI research previews and internal AMAs with the founding team.
• Travel + accommodation covered for any EduRankAI-sponsored campus event you host.
• Reimbursement for approved event expenses (venue, refreshments, basic materials) up to a per-event cap.
• Direct Slack + WhatsApp channel with the Outreach team for support, ideas, and escalations.
• Quarterly leadership cohort calls with founders + senior leadership.

You'll thrive here if: you already organize, write, post, present, or build community on your campus. You are comfortable being public about what you care about. You read carefully and you can write a clear update in five sentences.

You'll struggle here if: you only want a line on your resume. You prefer to be quiet about your work. You do not yet have a campus or community you can build inside.
responsibilities: Run 2–4 awareness events per semester on your campus — info sessions about EduRankAI roles, AquinTutor learning programmes, internships, hackathons, and research opportunities.
Be the first point of contact at your campus — answer questions from students considering applying, route them to the right resources, and surface their feedback to the team.
Author 1–2 short posts per month for the EduRankAI student blog or your campus community channels (in your name, edited by our team).
Build a small Ambassador team on your campus over the semester — recruit 2–3 student volunteers who help with events.
Coordinate with the EduRankAI Outreach team on a weekly basis — async written updates, a short monthly call.
Represent EduRankAI at relevant campus events, fests, hackathons, and conferences.
Help us hire from your campus — surface strong candidates for internships and full-time roles.
skills: Strong written communicator in English (regional language fluency is a major plus)
Comfort with public speaking + small-group facilitation
Active on at least one student community (campus club, hackathon circuit, student council, technical society, debate society, etc.)
Curious about AI / education / research / public-interest technology
Familiar with one social platform you actually use (LinkedIn, X, Instagram, Discord, WhatsApp communities, etc.)
eligibility: Currently enrolled at a recognised university or college (undergraduate or graduate), with at least one more semester to go.

You can point to at least one piece of public work you have done at your campus — an event you organized, a club you led, a post you wrote, a project you launched. Anything that shows you can move others on your campus.
You can commit at least 6–8 hours per week across the semester.
You write clearly and you read carefully. Submit a 200-word note in your application about what you would do in your first month as Campus Ambassador.
You are comfortable being publicly associated with EduRankAI on your campus social channels.

title: Chief Technology Officer
salary: INR 80 LPA - 1.5 Cr base + meaningful equity, refreshed annually on contribution.
about:
Most companies hire a CTO to manage what already exists. EduRankAI is hiring one to decide what should exist. You will own the technical arc across our research labs, our AI platform, the product surface, and the infrastructure underneath — and you will set the architectural decisions that determine what kind of company we become.

We are a frontier AI lab with a public-good mandate. The technology you choose, the people you hire, and the architecture you defend will shape whether EduRankAI ships systems the field studies or systems the field forgets.

You'll thrive here if: you've already been the senior-most technical person in a real company and want to build a second from cleaner ground. You can hold the research view and the production view in the same conversation. You write to think.

You'll struggle here if: you need a roadmap handed to you. You measure progress by org-chart depth. You prefer slides to commits.

Make it the place real decisions happen — not a rubber stamp.","Own the build-vs-buy decision at every layer, from cloud to inference to data pipelines.","Translate research breakthroughs from our lab (and the broader field) into shippable production systems.","Represent EduRankAI publicly on technical matters — papers, talks, technical disclosures, regulator briefings.","Be the technical conscience of the company: push back on shortcuts that compromise our public-good claims."]

title: Chief Learning Officer — AquinTutor
salary: INR 60 LPA - 1 Cr base + meaningful equity.
about:
AquinTutor is EduRankAI's flagship learner-facing product. The Chief Learning Officer owns whether the learning that happens inside it is real — measurable, transferable, generalisable — or just engagement theatre.

You will set the pedagogical framework, defend the assessment design against shortcuts, hire the curriculum and learning-science org, and own the public claims we make about learner outcomes. The CLO is also the person who pushes back hardest when product or growth wants to dilute rigour for retention.

You'll thrive here if: you have spent a career arguing for honesty in learning measurement, you can hold a research view and a product view in the same conversation, and you would rather ship one thing that works than ten that look like they do.

You'll struggle here if: you measure learning by minutes engaged, not skill transferred.

title: Chief Marketplace Officer

Own the marketplace product end-to-end. Supply-side acquisition, demand-side product, take-rate strategy, unit economics, partnerships. You are responsible for whether the marketplace works as a business — not just whether it ships features.

You'll thrive here if: you have run a marketplace at scale and you can describe the load-bearing unit-economic decision you got right. You read cohort dashboards for sport. You know that "more supply" is not always the answer.

You'll struggle here if: you have never had to choose between two cities. You think the model justifies the price. You manage by quarterly off-sites.

title: Editor-in-Chief — HEI Truth Report
about:
Lead the HEI Truth Report — India's most rigorous independent reporting on higher education. Set the editorial standard, defend the work under regulatory and reputational pressure, build the team that can do investigative reporting nobody else will.

You'll thrive here if: you have been a senior editor at a serious publication, you know what it means to defend a story against pressure, and you measure success by the reporting that wouldn't have existed without you.
responsibilities: ["Own the editorial standard and the methodology behind every story.","Hire the editorial + investigations team.","Defend reporting against legal + regulatory + commercial pressure.","Set the publication cadence and the long-form investigation backlog.","Own the public-facing identity of the HEI Truth Report.","Train the team in investigative methodology."]
skills: ["Senior editor at a serious publication","Investigative track record","Legal + regulatory literacy","Strong public writer","Direct, written-first communicator"]
eligibility: ["You have edited or led investigations at a serious publication.","You have defended a story against a lawsuit or formal pressure and won.","You have published work that produced a measurable policy or institutional change.","You write your own copy."]

title: Director — Investigations
about: Lead long-form investigative reporting for the HEI Truth Report. Senior reporters report to you. Two to four major investigations a year.
responsibilities: ["Lead the investigations team.","Author or co-author 2-4 major investigations a year.","Run the methodology bar — sources, evidence, fact-check.","Pair with data + legal on every investigation.","Train reporters on investigative methodology."]
skills: ["10+ years investigative reporting","Strong evidence + methodology discipline","Source network in higher education","Direct written communicator"]
eligibility: ["You have led at least three published investigations that produced visible institutional response.","You can defend an evidence chain to a defamation lawyer.","You have trained reporters into investigative work.","You write clean copy."]

title: Senior Data Journalist
about: Lead data-driven investigations for the HEI Truth Report. Public-records work, statistical analysis, visualisations that hold up to expert review.
responsibilities: ["Lead 4-6 data-driven stories a year.","Own the data methodology bar.","Pair with engineering on the public-facing data products.","Train reporters on data methods.","Defend numbers in fact-check + legal review."]
skills: ["Strong data journalism record","SQL + Python + R fluency","Statistical literacy","Public-records work","Strong writer"]
eligibility: ["You have published at least five data-driven investigations that survived scrutiny.","You can write a methodology note that a statistician would approve.","You read public records carefully.","You write clean visualisations."]

title: Senior HEI Infrastructure Engineer
about: Build the public-facing HEI data infrastructure — the queryable dataset, the visualisations, the truth-report distribution surface.
responsibilities: ["Own the public-facing HEI data product.","Build the queryable + visualised data layer.","Pair with editorial on what to publish and how.","Maintain data quality + lineage.","Mentor engineers."]
skills: ["Full-stack at production depth","Postgres + warehouse-tier OLAP","Data viz + storytelling","dbt + pipelines","Strong product instincts"]
eligibility: ["You have shipped a public-facing data product that has been live for 12+ months.","You can describe a data-quality bug you caught with a test you wrote.","You have an opinion on how to communicate uncertainty in data products.","You write clean code."]

title: Investigative Reporter
about:
Author 4-6 published investigations a year for the HEI Truth Report. Grow under the Director of Investigations and the Senior Data Journalist into a Senior Reporter role over 24-36 months.

The bar is serious investigative work — multi-source, evidence-rich, fact-checked, lawyer-reviewed. You will be expected to defend every load-bearing claim in writing and in person.
responsibilities: ["Author 4-6 published investigations a year.","Run source development + public-records work end-to-end.","Defend stories in fact-check, legal review, and editorial pushback.","Pair with the data team on quantitative claims.","Pair with the Director of Investigations on methodology.","Mentor reporting interns."]
skills: ["3-5 years reporting","Strong source-building","Excellent writer","Familiar with records work","Comfort with legal review"]
eligibility: ["You have published at least three substantial investigations under your byline that produced visible institutional response.","You can write a source-handling memo a colleague could follow.","You read public records carefully and can describe a story that turned on a specific record.","You write clean copy that survives editorial pushback.","You have defended at least one story against a legal threat or formal complaint."]

title: HEI Intern — Reporting
salary: Full-time stipend: up to ₹25,000/mo (India + developing economies) or USD 1,000/mo (developed economies) · Part-time: unpaid
about:
3 months on the HEI reporting team. Own a scoped reporting deliverable — a small investigation, a records review, or a fact-check on a major piece. Pair daily with Senior Reporters and the Director of Investigations.

The bar is "you publish something under your name that survives review".
responsibilities: ["Own one reporting deliverable in 3 months.","Pair daily with Senior Reporters.","Run records work yourself, under supervision.","Author a final piece at the level the publication would publish.","Present findings at end-of-internship review."]
skills: ["Strong writer","Curious about higher education","One shipped piece of journalism (student paper or organisational)","Comfort with public records","Detail-oriented"]
eligibility: ["You have published at least one piece of journalism, even at a student paper or organisational publication.","You write clean copy.","You read public records carefully.","You can describe a piece of reporting you admire and what makes it work.","You handle source relationships professionally."]

title: VP, Customer Success
about:
Own customer support across every EduRankAI product surface. AquinTutor learners, applicants, HEI subscribers, Karate.support users, the new consumer + marketplace surfaces. The team that determines whether users feel like they matter.

This is the role for someone who treats customer support as a serious craft, not a cost centre. You will set the standard, hire the team, build the tooling, and defend response-time + resolution-quality budgets against the easy temptation to dilute them.
responsibilities: ["Own customer support across every EduRankAI product.","Hire Director-level leadership across L1, L2, L3 support and customer success.","Set response-time + resolution-quality SLOs and defend them.","Own the support tooling roadmap with engineering.","Run quarterly customer-voice reviews into product.","Build the multi-lingual support coverage we need to serve India seriously."]
skills: ["Has run customer support at consumer scale","Strong product instincts","Multilingual programme leadership","Operational discipline","Direct, kind communicator"]
eligibility: ["You have led a customer support org of at least 50 across multiple languages.","You have shipped a support-quality improvement you can quantify.","You have an opinion on the difference between support and customer success.","You read tickets weekly."]

title: Director — Customer Success
about: Lead the customer success function. Account management for institutional + B2B partners (HEI subscribers, school + university partners for AquinTutor, fleet partners for the marketplace).
responsibilities: ["Lead CS for institutional + B2B partners.","Set the account management playbook.","Hire CS managers.","Quarterly business reviews with top accounts.","Surface churn signals back to product."]
skills: ["B2B customer success leadership","Strong communicator","Operational discipline","Numerate on account health"]
eligibility: ["You have led B2B CS at scale.","You have saved an at-risk account on purpose.","You read churn dashboards weekly.","You write clean account reviews."]

title: Director — Support Operations
about: Run day-to-day support operations across L1, L2, L3. Workforce planning, scheduling, escalation policy, quality assurance.
responsibilities: ["Run support ops across tiers.","Workforce planning + scheduling.","Own escalation policy.","Quality assurance + coaching.","Hire support managers."]
skills: ["Support ops at scale","WFM expertise","Quality + coaching discipline","Operational discipline"]

title: Support Specialist — Multilingual
about:
Handle L1 and L2 support across every EduRankAI product. The role is for support specialists who treat the work as a craft — who write clean tickets, who escalate with full context, and who carry multiple Indian languages comfortably.

You will pair with the Senior Support Engineer on L3 escalations and the Director of Support Operations on training. Grow into a Senior Support Engineer or CSM role over 24 months.
responsibilities: ["Handle L1 / L2 tickets across products.","Author KB articles for the patterns you see repeatedly.","Escalate to L3 with clean, complete context.","Run weekly trend reports for the Director.","Mentor junior support associates."]
skills: ["Multilingual (Hindi + at least one regional + English)","Strong writer","Product literacy across multiple products","Empathic communicator","Operational discipline"]
eligibility: ["You have handled L1 / L2 support at a real product for at least 18 months.","You write clean tickets and KB articles that other agents reuse.","You can describe a hard customer conversation you handled well, including what made it hard.","You can handle a difficult conversation in three languages without losing the room.","You have an opinion about response-time SLOs and you can defend it."]

eligibility: ["You have done customer-facing work, even informal, for at least 6 months.","You write clearly under pressure.","You handle hard conversations professionally and consistently.","You can describe a difficult support interaction you handled well."]
